summary: Today's sweep leans toward Games / criticism, Democracy / press freedom, Democracy / anti-fascism. The strongest items in this run are Middle East crisis live: Trump says he is ‘not at all’ worried about possible war crimes as his deadline for Iran nears, Members of neo-Nazi ‘active clubs’ join combat events at secretive Virginia compound, and Beyond Race: What Really Drives Wisconsin’s Achievement Gaps.

mainItems.title: Middle East crisis live: Trump says he is ‘not at all’ worried about possible war crimes as his deadline for Iran nears
mainItems.summary: Israel warns Iranians to immediately stop using trains or being near railway lines, saying it would ‘endanger’ their lives Here are some of the latest images coming in from the Middle East as the war continues in week six.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Worth watching for authoritarian drift, selective enforcement, and democratic norms.

mainItems.title: Members of neo-Nazi ‘active clubs’ join combat events at secretive Virginia compound
mainItems.summary: Licensed school teacher and one-time police officer among those participating in riot-style gatherings as experts warn of threat to public safety A network of militant neo-Nazi active clubs from around the US has been participating in…
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to state power, civil liberties, and anti-authoritarian concerns.

mainItems.title: Beyond Race: What Really Drives Wisconsin’s Achievement Gaps
mainItems.summary: For years, Wisconsin has held a troubling distinction in American education: the largest racial achievement gap in the nation.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Directly relevant to teaching, classroom practice, and educational judgment.

mainItems.title: Gemini is making it faster for distressed users to reach mental health resources
mainItems.summary: Google says it has updated Gemini to better direct users to get mental health resources during moments of crisis.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Touches trust, surveillance, and the private use of AI systems.

mainItems.title: "We're not doing zombie animals", says State of Decay 3 studio head, but you can turn a tea kettle into an undead-attracting bomb
mainItems.summary: If you have been keeping a candle lit for State Of Decay 3 since its 2020 reveal trailer purely for the prospect of fighting a zombie deer then, I'm afraid, it's time to blow that candle out.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Worth your time if you care about design, criticism, and not just hype.

mainItems.title: Wisconsin votes in supreme court race amid threat of midterm election attacks
mainItems.summary: Liberals see chance to strengthen majority in state where Trump and allies could try to overturn election results Wisconsin voters on Tuesday will select a state supreme court judge to replace an outgoing conservative in a race that could…

mainItems.title: Quantic Dream's cinematic Star Wars game Eclipse is reportedly still "years off from completion"
mainItems.summary: It's been over four years since Quantic Dream's Star Wars Eclipse was announced with a striking CGI trailer , which told us very little other than it was set in the High Republic era of George Lucas' galaxy far, far away.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Worth your time if you care about design, criticism, and not just hype.

mainItems.title: State of Decay 3 reveal trailer wasn't reflective of the actual game, says studio head
mainItems.summary: Studio head of State of Decay 3 developer Philip Holt has confirmed the 2020 reveal trailer was based entirely on "a concept" for the game, and a major inclusion in said trailer won't actually make it into the game: zombie animals.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Worth your time if you care about design, criticism, and not just hype.

mainItems.title: Trump administration ends some civil rights settlements backing trans students
mainItems.summary: Education department will no longer enforce schools from California to Delaware to comply with US civil rights law The US education department said on Monday it had terminated agreements that previous administrations reached with five…
mainItems.whyItMatters: Directly relevant to teaching, student agency, and school policy.

mainItems.title: Intel is going all-in on advanced chip packaging
mainItems.summary: Intel is hoping to cash in on the AI boom.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to the shape of current AI systems and where the tooling is moving.

mainItems.title: AI in the mental health care workforce is met with fear, pushback — and enthusiasm
mainItems.summary: Artificial intelligence tools that help mental health therapists take notes and keep records are quickly entering the marketplace. But some question the safety of AI in mental health care delivery.

mainItems.title: Building AI bots becomes the latest viral craze in China
mainItems.summary: A fascination with AI bots, made with a program called OpenClaw, is sweeping China.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Interesting where engineering, science, and tech infrastructure collide.

trailers.summary: Is love truly blind? The pods of 'Love Is Blind: Poland' open on May 6th. Watch on Netflix: https://www.netflix.

trailers.whyItMatters: Relevant to your current interest graph.

new.title: Scavengers Reign
new.why: Animated speculative sci-fi with strong worldbuilding and a weirder imagination than most prestige SF.

new.title: Sugar
new.why: Noir detective structure with a genre wrinkle and a sensibility that feels adjacent to your crime/strangeness overlap.

new.title: The Lazarus Project
new.why: Time-loop espionage, moral pressure, and speculative plotting — very plausible fit.

new.title: Bodies
new.why: Time-spanning conspiracy/mystery structure that fits your taste for long-arc puzzle storytelling.

older.title: Counterpart
older.why: Probably the single cleanest “James show you may have missed”: espionage + parallel-world sci-fi + adult competence.

older.title: Patriot
older.why: Dry, strange, melancholy spy storytelling with a very distinct voice.

older.title: Utopia
older.why: Paranoid conspiracy energy, formal boldness, and a willingness to get strange and ugly.

older.title: Rubicon
older.why: A low-key conspiracy thriller that leans heavily on intelligence work, paranoia, and institutional atmosphere.

shared.title: Bad Sisters
shared.why: Darkly funny, well-observed, and ensemble-driven in a way that plausibly overlaps with Hacks / Derry Girls / Mare of Easttown energy.

shared.title: Detectorists
shared.why: Gentle, funny, humane, and specific — a softer shared-watch possibility.

shared.title: Astrid et Raphaëlle
shared.why: French procedural with a strong central duo and exactly the kind of foreign-crime appeal Maggie may share with you.

shared.title: Somebody Somewhere
shared.why: A warmer character-driven recommendation if the shared overlap leans more Bear / Good Place than pure detective work.
